page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

The x86_64 architecture of the Intel Xeon CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions (avx512f and avx512_vnni), alongside cryptography acceleration via AES. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates mid-range performance. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1435.7 and a multi-core score of 11480.59. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 640.72 GB/sec, which drops to 72.14 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 45.09 GB/sec.

For application workloads, the ecs.hfr6.3xlarge delivers 11.09 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and handles static web serving throughput at 9.73 GB/sec. Software compilation via Clang scores 10374.0 on Geekbench, while database operations reach a PassMark score of 4450.48. Large language model inference speeds are moderate for small 135M models, processing 747.78 prompt tokens per second, but drop significantly to 17.27 prompt tokens per second for medium 7B models.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise application servers, and moderate data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth instead. Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 11480.59 and a Redis SET throughput of 11.09 million operations per second. Its Intel Xeon processor supports AVX-512 instructions, aiding workloads like cryptography and vector calculations. This hardware profile makes the instance cost-effective for memory-intensive applications, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise application hosting, where high memory-to-CPU ratios are required without the need for local NVMe disks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 12 physical cores running at 3.1 GHz.
96.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio.
Supports AVX-512 and AES instruction sets for accelerated vector and cryptographic processing.
Delivers 11.09 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
